citations.text: Mesoudi, A. (2011). Cultural Evolution: How Darwinian Theory Can Explain Human Culture and Synthesize the Social Sciences.

en.ai: A search or optimization process that favors candidates scoring higher against an objective function.
en.biology: Differential reproductive success among heritable variants.
en.culture: Differential persistence or spread of cultural variants — ideas, practices, or artifacts — based on their fit to a social or environmental context.
en.education: Choosing among available instructional pathways or resources.

examples: A cultural practice being adopted more widely because it improves group cohesion.
A genetic algorithm retaining higher-fitness candidate solutions across generations.
nonExamples: The random generation of a new variant (see Variation, Innovation).
